pages.raw_text:
Attorneys around the courthouse
are specializing as to whether
James j. Hill will succeed in hav-
ing the papers wanted by the
grand jury from the Great North
.ern general offices in st. Paul sent
to Spokane.


Some think he will, while a great
many more are of the opinion that
the directors will hold against Hill.
If meeting is held, and that for
this reason the papers will not be
produced.


Prosecutor Pugh is pleased that
Hill has gone on record by agree
ing to urge th.t the papers be
sent, for if the required documents
are not then forthcoming it will
be up to Mr.. Hill to explain.


Some lawyers recall with some
interest the trial OF suit a num-
Der of years ago when Hill was
asked II he knew who owned the
Great Northern railroad and he re-
sponded that he did not know.
When further questioned as to who
was building the road Hill like
wise was at loss to explain. and
said he did not know.
